title: TEMPORARY: instrumenting for 15488
body:
Trying to catch the wiley metacopy flake: add a debug
condition to run_podman, in system tests, to log all
instances in which output includes the metacopy warning.
The idea is to detect the very first time it happens,
and see what is triggering it.

Signed-off-by: Ed Santiago <santiago@redhat.com>
```release-note
None
```

comments.body: Favor request: if the flake triggers, please don't anyone rerun it! I'm not working today, or at least trying to respect some portion of my nonwork day, and might not be around to watch this CI run until later. TIA.
Theoretically the Metacopy error should be gone with the merging up containers/storage fix from @giuseppe .

I'd still like to understand #15488, because it seems to happen only in one specific test, and there's no `--storage-opt` anywhere near it. Something is happening, right in the middle of tests, that would be really nice to understand.

Before we do: I tried to grok the c/c fix but couldn't. Is the `metacopy` warning completely gone? If so, then the flake is gone too, and this PR will be a NOP. If the `metacopy` warning still exists, and it's just the `--storage-opt` trigger that is fixed, then we might still see the metacopy flake and this should merge. Who knows the answer?

(FWIW the metacopy flake hasn't happened in a few days)

> Who knows the answer?

@giuseppe will know :+1:
it is completely gone if it was caused by using a mount program.  If it was caused by something else, we'd still see it.  So I think this PR makes sense to have
